GPU-first canvas backend. Uses SDL2's hardware-accelerated renderer (Metal on macOS, Direct3D on Windows, OpenGL elsewhere) for every primitive that maps directly to it:

  • clear -> SDL_RenderClear
  • drawRect -> SDL_RenderFillRect
  • drawLine -> SDL_RenderDrawLine
  • compositeSubCanvas -> SDL_RenderCopy (shared with SdlCanvas)

Primitives that aren't direct renderer ops (rounded rectangles, circles, anti-aliased shapes, text) are rasterized once with Pixie into a cached SDL_Texture keyed by (kind, dims, color) and then drawn on every subsequent frame via SDL_RenderCopy. After the first frame, even those primitives are effectively zero-CPU.

When SdlCanvas is the "Pixie-on-CPU then upload" path, GpuCanvas is the "SDL renderer is the truth" path. Both expose the same Canvas surface; the platform runner picks one. Pick GpuCanvas for UIs whose pixels mostly come from solid rects, images, and cached layers. Pick SdlCanvas when you need Pixie's high-quality anti-aliased path rendering for every primitive.

Imports

../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/geometry, glyph_atlas

Types

GpuCanvas = ref object of Canvas
  window*: WindowPtr
  renderer*: RendererPtr
  fonts*: Table[string, Font]
  defaultFont*: Font
  shapeCache*: Table[ShapeKey, TexturePtr]
  atlas*: GlyphAtlas
  tx*, ty*: float32
  stateStack*: seq[(float32, float32)]
SDL_Renderer-based canvas. Direct hardware draws for rects / lines / clears; cached textures (also drawn on the renderer) for paths and shapes that the SDL renderer can't express natively. 
GpuSubCanvas = ref object of Canvas
  parent*: GpuCanvas
  texture*: TexturePtr
Off-screen surface for a RepaintBoundary under a GpuCanvas. Backed by a render-target texture; drawing inside it issues GPU primitives directly into the target.

Procs

proc disposeSubCanvas(s: GpuSubCanvas) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Releases the render-target texture. Safe to call multiple times. Call when the owning RepaintBoundary unmounts. 
proc newGpuCanvas(window: WindowPtr; renderer: RendererPtr; w, h: int;
                  defaultFontPath: string = ""): GpuCanvas {....raises: [],
    tags: [ReadIOEffect, RootEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}

Builds a GpuCanvas. The caller owns the window and renderer; the canvas only uses them. Fonts may be loaded lazily by the glyph atlas.

Inputs:

  • window, renderer: live SDL2 handles.
  • w, h: surface size.
  • defaultFontPath: optional TTF path. Empty leaves text rendering off until a glyph atlas is installed.

Output: a GpuCanvas ready to be painted onto.

proc present(c: GpuCanvas) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Flushes the renderer to the window. Call once per frame after all draw calls have run. No CPU upload step; everything is already on the GPU. 
proc trimShapeCache(c: GpuCanvas; maxEntries: int = 256) {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Caps the shape cache size. The cache otherwise grows unboundedly for apps that draw many unique (size, color, radius) shape combinations. Call occasionally (e.g. once per second from the runner) or when memory pressure is detected.

Methods

                  fill: uint32) {....raises: [KeyError, PixieError],
                                  tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
method drawCircle(s: GpuSubCanvas; center: geom.Offset; radius: float32;
                  fill: uint32) {....raises: [KeyError, PixieError],
                                  tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
method drawLine(s: GpuSubCanvas; p0, p1: geom.Offset; color: uint32;
                width: float32) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
method drawRect(c: GpuCanvas; r: geom.Rect; fill: uint32) {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
method drawRect(s: GpuSubCanvas; r: geom.Rect; fill: uint32) {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
method drawRRect(c: GpuCanvas; r: geom.RRect; fill: uint32) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, PixieError], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
method drawRRect(s: GpuSubCanvas; r: geom.RRect; fill: uint32) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, PixieError], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
method drawText(c: GpuCanvas; text: string; pos: geom.Offset; color: uint32;
                fontSize: float32; fontFamily: string) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, PixieError], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Goes through the glyph atlas: first draw of a given (text, fontHash, size, color) rasterizes once, subsequent draws are a single GPU RenderCopy of the cached texture. 
method drawText(s: GpuSubCanvas; text: string; pos: geom.Offset; color: uint32;
                fontSize: float32; fontFamily: string) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, PixieError], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Routes through the parent canvas's glyph atlas. 
method restore(c: GpuCanvas) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
method restore(s: GpuSubCanvas) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
method rotate(c: GpuCanvas; radians: float32) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Not supported on the GPU fast path. Wrap rotating subtrees in repaintBoundary for the same reason as scale. 
method rotate(s: GpuSubCanvas; radians: float32) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
method save(c: GpuCanvas) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
method save(s: GpuSubCanvas) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
method scale(c: GpuCanvas; sx, sy: float32) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Not supported on the GPU fast path. Widgets that need scaling should wrap themselves in a repaintBoundary so the scaled render is captured into a sub-canvas, which then composites back with a cleanly scaled destination rect. 
method scale(s: GpuSubCanvas; sx, sy: float32) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
method translate(c: GpuCanvas; dx, dy: float32) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
method translate(s: GpuSubCanvas; dx, dy: float32) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
